summary: sensivity ships obfuscated Windows game-cheat with hidden persistence, brand impersonation, and HWID exfiltration
details: On require()/import the package's declared main (launcher.js) eval()s a heavily obfuscated 78 KB blob (server.obf.js, obfuscator.io string-array RC4 + control-flow flattening) and loads a bundled 6.8 MB Windows PE native addon (sens.node) whose strings reveal a game-cheat (Aimbot, Triggerbot, Teleport, bone offsets LeftFoot/LeftHand/Head). The package self-describes only as 'Mobile Control Panel'. On load, launcher.js writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run key named 'OneDriveUpdate' that invokes wscript.exe against a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which silently respawns node launcher.js with a hidden window; the Node process additionally sets process.title='Runtime Broker' to masquerade as a legitimate Windows process. The deobfuscated server collects a hardware fingerprint (HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid via PowerShell + os.hostname() + C: volume serial, sha256'd) and POSTs it with a nonce to a remote LICENSE endpoint (URL assembled from obfuscated chunks, path ending in 'eck.php'). launcher.js installs no-op uncaughtException/unhandledRejection handlers to swallow all errors. Multiple independent installer-harm mechanisms are present: obfuscated remote-controlled execution at module load, persistent autostart via Microsoft-brand impersonation, off-host transmission of unique machine identifiers, and shipment of unauthorized memory-modification code that will trigger AV/EDR and TOS violations on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
